name: baoyu-markdown-to-html
description: Converts Markdown to styled HTML with WeChat-compatible themes. Supports code highlighting, math, PlantUML, footnotes, alerts, and infographics. Use when user asks for "markdown to html", "convert md to html", "md转html", or needs styled HTML output from markdown.
capabilities: Converts Markdown files to styled HTML with inline CSS
Supports multiple themes optimized for WeChat (default, grace, simple)
Renders code blocks with syntax highlighting
Generates diagrams from PlantUML code blocks
Downloads and embeds remote images locally
author: jimliu
securitySummary: This is a legitimate markdown-to-HTML converter skill. The static findings are false positives - external commands invoke the rendering script and external tools (PlantUML, Ruby) for markdown extensions, network calls fetch external resources needed for rendering (images, diagram servers), and filesystem operations are standard read/write for file conversion. No malicious intent or data exfiltration detected.
valueStatement: Transforms plain Markdown files into beautifully styled HTML with WeChat-optimized themes, code highlighting, math equations, and diagram support.
limitations: External tools (Ruby, PlantUML) needed for some extensions
Complex diagrams may require PlantUML server access
WeChat theme optimizations may not suit all use cases

useCases.targetUser: Content creators publishing technical articles to WeChat
useCases.title: WeChat Article Publishing
useCases.description: Convert Markdown articles to WeChat-compatible HTML for publishing to Official Accounts with styled formatting and code highlighting.

useCases.title: Documentation Conversion
useCases.description: Transform Markdown documentation files into standalone HTML with beautiful styling and diagram support.

useCases.targetUser: Technical bloggers needing portable HTML output
useCases.title: Blog Post Export
useCases.description: Convert Markdown blog posts to styled HTML with math equations and syntax-highlighted code blocks.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Markdown Conversion
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert the file README.md to HTML using the default theme
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick conversion of a simple markdown file

promptTemplates.title: WeChat-Optimized Output
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert article.md to HTML with the grace theme, optimized for WeChat publication
promptTemplates.scenario: Preparing content for WeChat Official Account

promptTemplates.title: With Code Highlighting
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert tutorial.md to HTML with the simple theme, include code block syntax highlighting

promptTemplates.title: Keep Original Title
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert doc.md to HTML using the default theme and keep the original title in the output

bestPractices: Use the default theme for WeChat articles as it's optimized for that platform
Keep markdown files in the same directory as referenced images for proper embedding
Test the output in WeChat editor before publishing to catch formatting issues
antiPatterns: Do not use this for converting sensitive documents - HTML is not encrypted
Avoid converting very large markdown files as processing may be slow
Do not expect perfect rendering of complex JavaScript-heavy markdown extensions

faq.question: What themes are available?
faq.answer: Three themes are available: default (classic WeChat style), grace (elegant with shadows and rounded cards), and simple (modern minimalist).

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: You need Bun runtime installed. The skill will use npx/bun to execute the conversion script.

faq.question: Can I use this for WeChat articles?
faq.answer: Yes, the default and grace themes are specifically optimized for WeChat Official Account articles.

faq.question: How are remote images handled?
faq.answer: Remote images are automatically downloaded and embedded with local paths in the HTML for offline viewing.

faq.question: What markdown extensions are supported?
faq.answer: Code highlighting, math equations (KaTeX), PlantUML diagrams, footnotes, alerts, and custom infographics.

faq.question: Can I customize the output theme?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide custom CSS variables through the EXTEND.md configuration file.
